observations.recommendation: We recommended the agency to strengthen its internal control to safeguard its assets and to check the accuracy and reliability of its accounting data. We further recommend the Accounting Office and the CGSO to: book up supplies, materials, and equipment and relief goods procured out of the agency’s fund under the appropriate Inventory and PPE account. Except for the PPE account, issuance for distribution to end users/beneficiaries shall be supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and taken up under the appropriate expense account.

We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10) days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in accordance with government rules and regulations, attached is the COA Circular 97-61 complete with sample form of monthly report of fuel consumption.

We recommended that the LCE: 1. Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government machinery subjected to repairs. 2. Require that the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material be prepared by the Supply Officer and approved by the LCE. And 3. Direct the Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should: a. Cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all completed, on-going and those that are to be implemented projects/programs/activities for the year 2020 and prior years, containing all necessary information such as i. Project Name; ii. Implementing Unit/Office/Division; iii. Brief description of the projects/programs/activities; iv. Contractor or Supplier; v. Mode of procurement; vi. Funding source; vii. Cost of approved budget; viii. Project duration including start and completion dates; ix. Location; b. Impose strict implementation of the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 – 004; and c. Always provide the Audit Team with update information as to the status of the publicity of projects/programs/activities in order to allow timely validation of the same.

We have recommended the following: a. For the GSO to conduct physical count for all PPE and prepare property card (PC) as prescribed by NGAS manual, and b. Coordination between the GSO and the Accounting Office and ensure that their records on all PPE Municipality tally.

Recommendation:
We recommended the (a) Municipal Accountant to submit subsidiary ledger for cash
in bank account; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer to present to the Auditors Office the
updated Cashbook.

Recommendation:
We recommended the (a) Municipal Treasurer to submit monthly RCD to the
Municipal Accountant for recording purposes and submit to the Auditors Office
within 10 days of the ensuing month; and (b) Municipal Treasurer s to maintain
cashbook for cash in treasury accounts to records all collections and remittances
made.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF together with the
RAAF to the Audit Team not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month.

Recommendation:
We recommended the (a) Municipal Accountant to consistently record the inventories
in their respective asset accounts and recognized as expense the value of issued
inventories by requiring the MGSO to submit SSMI promptly; and (b) the MGSO to
create an inventory Team to conduct physical count of inventories and submit the
RPCI in the prescribed format and date to the audit team.

RECOMMENDATION:
We have reiterated our previous years audit recommendation to the Management as
follows (a) To conduct Physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type
annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) and submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than
January 31 of each year; and (b) To maintain a complete, updated and reconciled
PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards by the Accounting and CGS Office
respectively.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to conduct
reconciliation of their reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended the (a) Accountant to submit the required DRRM utilization report on or
before the 15th day of the ensuing month; (b) MDRRMO to submit the required annual
LDRRMFIP to the Auditors Office; (c) the MSWD to submit monthly reports on the receipt
and distribution/utilization/issuances of in-kind donation; and (d) the Accountant to transfer
the unexpended DRRMF to the STF under the account of Trust Liability DRRM.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management (a) the submission of reviewed GAD annual
Accomplishment Report; (b) to consider the establishment of a GAD office to
maintain a focused, concentrated, and coordinated response in the administration,
planning, implementation, and reporting of GAD related PPAs; and (c) to submit
DILG approved GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2021.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to (a) submit immediately the subsidiary
ledger to substantiate balances in the together with the Notes to Financial Statement to
the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular; (b) to submit annual Financial Statement
on or before February 14 of each year.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to
submit: 1) Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amount and 2) Statement of
Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) to the Auditors Office;
and to direct the (b) Municipal Accountant to submit 1) Statement of Cash Flow, b)
Subsidiary Ledgers; and c) Adjusting and Closing Journal Entry Vouchers,

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC 125 also known as the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities, likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and make the necessary adjusting entry as follows:

To create an Inventory Committee and conduct Physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; To maintain a complete, updated and reconciled PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards by the Accounting and CGS Office respectively; and To comply with COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 on the Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies.

We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended Management to comply with the provision and guidelines stipulated in the DILG - DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 - 001 dated February 22, 2017 and the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry and to reflect its related depreciation.

We recommended that at the end of each year the Municipal Accountant should prepare the journal entry to record the transfer of the unexpended portion of LDRRMF from General Fund to the Trust Fund and to open a separate depository account exclusively for the DRRM Fund in accordance with Section 310 of RA 7160 and Section 3 of COA Circular 92 - 382.

We recommended Management to appropriate at least 5% of the annual budget for GAD and to maximize its utilization through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which GAD funds were provided. We also urged the Management to abide by the GAD annual plan and budget of the agency to achieve its objective of strengthening public information on GAD issues and policies.

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC 125 also
known as the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities, likewise full disclosure
policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend further to submit a written
explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA
9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and
revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and make the necessary adjusting entry as follows:
Prior Period Adjustment 170,194.00
Due to NGA 170,194.00
To take up the ending balance of the BGCM account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:
a. Caused the immediate submission of a complete list of all completed, on-going and those that are to be implemented projects/programs/activities for the year 2020 and prior years, containing all necessary information relative thereto, such as:
i. Project Name
ii. Implementing Unit/Office/Division
iii. Brief description of the projects/programs/activities
iv. Contractor or Supplier
v. Mode of procurement
Vi. Funding source
Vii. Cost of approved budget
viii. Project duration including start and completion dates
1X. Location;
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger Accounts for all funds as required = under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.

We have recommended the following:
a. For the GSO to conduct physical count for all PPE and prepare property card (PC) as prescribed by NGAS manual, and
b. Coordination between the GSO and the Accounting Office and ensure that their recor ds on all PPE Municipality tally.

We recommended that the Local — Chief Executive require the concerned official to submit a copy of the annual GAD Accomplishment report together with the monthly = monitoring report of Program/Projects and Activities for infrastructure project to the COA Audit Team in conformance with the provisions stated in COA Circular 2014-001 and COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA’s report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:
a. Caused the immediate submission of a complete list of all completed, on-going and those
that are to be implemented projects/programs/activities for the year 2020 and prior
years, containing all necessary information relative thereto, such as:
i. Project Name
ii. Implementing Unit/Office/Division
iii. Brief description of the projects/programs/activities
iv. Contractor or Supplier
v. Mode of procurement
vi. Funding source
vii. Cost of approved budget
viii. Project duration including start and completion dates
ix. Location;
b. Imposed strict implementation of the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 – 004; and
c. Always provide the Audit Team with update information as to the status of the publicity of
projects/programs/activities/ in order to allow timely validation of the same.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Subsidiary Schedule of
General Ledger Accounts for all funds as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular
No. 2010-001.

RECOMMENDATION:
We have recommended the following:
a. For the GSO to conduct physical count for all PPE and prepare property card
(PC) as prescribe by NGAS manual, and
b. Coordination between the GSO and the Accounting Office and ensure that their
records on all PPE owned by the Municipality tally.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the
requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the
audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the
Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re:
Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be
properly observed.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and all Department Heads to adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials provided under Chapter 7 of the NGAS manual; and
All Purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as inventories under appropriate account.

We therefore recommended that the Accountable Officer:
Shall secure the correct cash book form and established the Cash Disbursing Officer to commence from last examination, and
Keep an updated balance of cashbook which shall be made available to cash management and COA in the conduct of audit of cash and accounts of the Accountable Officer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned official to submit a copy of the annual GAD Accomplishment report together with the monthly monitoring report of Program/Projects and Activities for infrastructure project to the COA Audit Team in conformance with the provisions stated in COA Circular 2014-001 and COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:


            We recommended that  the  head  of  agency  shall  instruct  the
Accountant to    prepare  the  Bank  Reconciliation  Statement  (BRS)  on  a
monthly basis  in        accordance  with  COA  Circular  No.  96-011  dated
October 2, 1996 for auditor’s     immediate  review and verification and  to
facilitate    the    immediate    preparation    of         the    necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or  errors  or      other
reconciling items requiring corrections in  order  to  reflect  the  correct
balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

RECOMMENDATION:


            We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal
      Engineer  submit   the   list   of   all   on   –   going   government
      projects/programs/activities and those  that  are  to  be  implemented
      during the year and submit monthly  monitoring  PPA’s  report  of  the
      agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

Direct the persons responsible to settle  the audit  suspension
      through compliance with the requirements indicated in  the  Notice  of
      Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must
      settle the same in accordance with COA  Circular  No.  2009-006  dated
      September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules  and  Regulations  on
      the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular  No.  2012-001  dated
      June  14,  2012.  Re:  Revised  Documentary  Requirements  for  Common
      Government Transaction must be properly observed.

Observe properly COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 that prescribed the deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, and reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from January-November, 2012 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

We further recommended that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.

We, therefore recommend that the agency’s officials and employees shall strictly adhere the pertinent section under P.D 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from-December, 2015 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.
